Label: Admin With Third Party Token
Description__c: Fires when a user assigned the System Administrator profile holds any OauthToken. Admin-issued OAuth tokens combine the highest org privileges with credentials that often live outside MFA, so each grant should be reviewed individually until a trusted-publisher allowlist is configured.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Confirm with the admin user that the connected app is required for their role and is published by a trusted vendor. If not, revoke the OauthToken in Setup > Connected Apps OAuth Usage and rotate any related API credentials. If approved, document the exception and prepare the app for inclusion in the future trusted-publisher allowlist.

Label: API Volume Spike
Description__c: Flags users whose API call volume in the last twenty-four hours exceeds twice their ninety-day trailing average, with a noise floor of one hundred calls per day. Sudden API spikes on a previously-quiet user often correlate with credential theft, runaway integration scripts, or unauthorized bulk extracts.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Identify the connected app or client driving the spike, confirm the integration owner authorized the change, and inspect IP and user-agent for the burst window. If unexplained, revoke the user's active sessions and OAuth tokens, throttle the integration user, and follow up with the connected-app owner.

Label: Baseline New App
Description__c: Flags newly issued OauthToken rows whose AppName is absent from the user's warmed-up baseline App_Set__c. Users granting consent to apps they have never authorized before is the dominant signal of an OAuth phishing campaign, especially when paired with surge or broad-scope findings on the same application.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Inspect the connected app: publisher, scopes requested, install base, and creation date. Revoke the user's token if the app is unknown or scope is excessive. If multiple users in a short window authorized the same unknown app, treat as a campaign and block the connected app org-wide.

Label: Baseline New Country
Description__c: Flags successful logins from a country that is not present in the user's warmed-up baseline Country_Set__c. A new login country for a user with established travel patterns is a strong indicator of credential reuse, session hijacking, or unannounced travel that should be verified out-of-band.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Contact the user out-of-band (phone, established chat) to verify travel. If the login was not theirs, expire all active sessions, reset the password, and reset MFA factors. Review LoginHistory and OauthToken records around the time of the login for further compromise.

Label: Baseline New Ip24
Description__c: Flags successful logins from a /24 IP block that is not in the user's warmed-up baseline Ip24_Set__c. New IP ranges typically indicate new VPN endpoints, residential moves, travel, or compromise. The /24 granularity reduces noise from carrier NAT and corporate IP shuffles while still surfacing meaningful change.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Correlate the IP with the user's known VPN providers and corporate egress ranges. If the IP is unfamiliar, contact the user to verify. If the login is suspicious, expire sessions, rotate the password, and review surrounding activity for lateral movement or data exfiltration.

Label: Baseline Off Hours
Description__c: Flags successful logins occurring at an hour of day that represents less than two percent of the user's historical activity. Off-hours logins often indicate attacker access from a different time zone using stolen credentials. Low severity because off-hours work is common and benign in isolation.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Triage in batches. If multiple off-hours findings cluster around the same user, correlate with country and IP baselines for stronger signal. For isolated findings, confirm with the user during their next normal-hours session.

Label: Bulk API From New Client
Description__c: Flags Bulk API jobs initiated by a user from a client name (or source) that user has never used in the prior thirty days. A new Bulk API client on a previously-quiet user is a strong indicator of credential theft, unauthorized integration deployment, or a rogue developer side-loading a new tool.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Identify the new client name and confirm whether the user authorized a new integration. If unexpected, revoke the user's OAuth tokens, freeze active sessions, and inspect Bulk API job results for what data was extracted. If legitimate, document the new client in the user's integration baseline so future runs do not re-fire.

Label: Failed Login Burst
Description__c: Detects source IP addresses producing a burst of failed LoginHistory rows in the past hour. A high concentration of failures from one IP is a strong indicator of a password-spray or brute-force attempt against multiple users.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Confirm the source IP is not a known corporate egress or test harness. If it is hostile, add the IP range to Network Access restrictions and notify any users whose accounts were targeted. Review their LoginHistory for successful logins from the same IP.

Label: Failed MFA Burst
Description__c: Flags any single user with three or more MFA verification failures in the last 24 hours. Repeated MFA failures for one user are a strong signal of an MFA fatigue or push-bombing attack, an attacker testing stolen credentials, or a compromised session attempting to complete a second factor.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Contact the user out of band to confirm whether they were attempting to sign in. If not, reset their password, revoke active sessions and tokens, and require MFA re-enrollment. If yes, document the cause (lost device, slow network) and consider strengthening the user's MFA method.

Label: IP Restriction Removed
Description__c: Fires when SetupAuditTrail entries in the last 7 days reference deletion or removal of a Login IP Range. Login IP Ranges are a network-layer compensating control, so each removal is treated as a high-severity change pending review.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Open Setup > Profiles or Setup > Network Access and verify whether the removed Login IP Range was replaced by an equivalent control. If the removal was unauthorized, restore the IP range from version control or your change-management ticket. Confirm with the change author and attach the approval to the finding.

Label: Impossible Travel
Description__c: Detects pairs of successful logins for the same user from two countries that are physically too far apart for the elapsed time to be plausible. A confirmed match indicates either credential compromise or unsanitized VPN usage and warrants immediate session termination.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Contact the user to confirm whether both logins were them. If not, expire their password, revoke active sessions, force MFA re-enrollment, and review recent activity for unauthorized changes. If the user confirms legitimate VPN use, document the source IP range and consider a network allowlist.

Label: Inactive User Login Success
Description__c: Flags any successful login in the last 24 hours where the User.IsActive flag is false. A successful login against an inactive user typically means the account was temporarily reactivated for an attack, credentials were used in the window before disable propagated, or a service account is misclassified. Each occurrence is high signal and warrants immediate investigation.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Immediately revoke active sessions and OAuth tokens for the user. Reset the password and ensure IsActive remains false. Review who reactivated the account, recent admin activity, and any data accessed during the session. If the user is a legitimate service account, reclassify it and document the exception.

Label: LoginAs Outside Hours
Description__c: Flags LoginAs impersonation events (admin assuming another user's session) that occurred outside business hours, defined as 08:00 through 18:00. Off-hours admin impersonation is a high-signal anomaly because legitimate support sessions almost always happen during normal business hours.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Identify the impersonating admin and confirm the session was authorized (ticket, on-call rotation, scheduled maintenance). If unexplained, revoke the admin's session, rotate credentials, and review actions taken during the impersonation window in Setup Audit Trail and field history.

Label: MFA Disabled For User
Description__c: Scans recent SetupAuditTrail entries for Multi-Factor Authentication being disabled for individual users. Disabling MFA reverts a key compensating control, so each occurrence should be reviewed and either restored or formally approved.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Verify with the change author that disabling MFA was intentional and approved. If not, re-enable the MFA requirement for the affected user and review the actor's recent admin activity. If approved, attach the change ticket to the finding's resolution notes.

Label: Mass Report Export
Description__c: Flags users who exported more than fifty reports in the last twenty-four hours, based on ReportExport rows in EventLogFile. High-volume report export by a single user is a leading indicator of data exfiltration, especially when correlated with login anomalies, new OAuth tokens, or off-hours activity on the same user.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Confirm with the user (or their manager) whether the export burst was expected. If unexpected, freeze the user, revoke active sessions and OAuth tokens, review the exported reports for sensitive data, and notify the data-owner team. If the burst aligns with a known migration or audit, document the exception and tune the threshold.

Label: ModifyAllData Granted
Description__c: Flags PermissionSet assignments in the last 24 hours where the assigned permission set carries Modify All Data or View All Data. These permissions are functionally equivalent to admin access for the affected user and should be reviewed each time they are granted.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Confirm the assignment was approved by the user's manager and that the user has a documented business need. If not, remove the assignment and review the assignee's recent activity. If the grant is legitimate, attach the approval reference to the finding's resolution notes.

Label: New App Surge
Description__c: Flags newly registered connected apps that have been authorized by more than five distinct users within the last 24 hours. A sudden surge of consent grants on a brand-new app is a classic indicator of an OAuth phishing campaign or a viral integration spreading faster than security review can keep up with.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Pull the list of users who authorized the app and confirm with a sample whether the consent was intentional. If the app is unrecognized or unapproved, block it, revoke its active tokens, and notify the affected users. If legitimate, document the approval and adjust scope policy if needed.

Label: New ConnectedApp Broad Scope
Description__c: Flags newly observed connected apps that request broad OAuth scopes (api, refresh_token, full, web). A new third-party app with these scopes can read or modify nearly any data the installing user can see, so each one should be reviewed and either approved or blocked.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Open the Connected App in Setup. Confirm with the installer that the app is legitimate and required. If not, revoke active tokens, block the app, and inform the installer. Document the approval in the finding's resolution notes.

Label: Orphan ConnectedApp
Description__c: Flags connected apps that still hold active OAuth tokens but whose installer user is inactive or unknown. An orphaned connected app continues to act under credentials with no owner, allowing stale automation, departed employees' integrations, or unattributed access to persist past offboarding.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Identify the business owner of the connected app. If the app is still required, reassign it to an active owner and document the change. If not, revoke active tokens, block the app from future authorization, and remove it from the inventory. Record the disposition in the finding's resolution notes.

Label: Privileged PermSet Granted
Description__c: Flags PermissionSet assignments granted in the last 24 hours where the permission set carries Modify All Data, Manage Users, or has a label containing "admin". These permissions effectively elevate the assignee to a privileged role, and every grant should be tied to a documented business reason.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Confirm with the actor and the assignee's manager that the grant was approved and the assignee has a documented need. If not, remove the assignment and review the actor's recent admin activity for further unauthorized changes. If legitimate, attach the approval reference to the finding's resolution notes.

Label: Session Timeout Extended
Description__c: Fires when SetupAuditTrail entries in the last 7 days mention Session Timeout. The Display field stores the change as free text, so this rule conservatively raises a finding on every match; triage must confirm whether the new value exceeds the org's policy (typically > 8 hours).
Remediation_Guidance__c: Inspect the audit entry's Display text to confirm the new session timeout value. If it exceeds the org policy, revert via Setup > Session Settings or the affected profile and confirm with the change author. If the new value is compliant, dismiss the finding with a note referencing the policy threshold.

Label: Stale OAuth Token
Description__c: Fires when an OauthToken's LastUsedDate is more than 90 days old. Tokens left dormant often correspond to departed employees, decommissioned integrations, or shadow-IT trials, and continue to widen the OAuth attack surface until revoked.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Confirm with the token owner whether the integration is still needed. If not, revoke the token in Setup > Connected Apps OAuth Usage. If still required but rarely used, document the business justification and consider tightening the connected app's IP restrictions or session settings.

Label: Unknown ConnectedApp Granted
Description__c: Fires when an active OauthToken references a connected app (AppMenuItem.ApplicationId) that is not present in OAuth_App_Inventory__c. Tokens against un-cataloged apps indicate a user-driven grant to software that the security team has never reviewed.
Remediation_Guidance__c: Identify the application from the payload, contact the subject user to confirm the grant was intentional and business-justified, and either add the app to the OAuth inventory after review or revoke the OauthToken via Setup > Connected Apps OAuth Usage. Repeat for each token tied to the same application.
